goal: Fix mainline init behavior for existing repos with missing local identity

summary.title: Fix init local identity bootstrap
summary.what: Adjusted init so an existing mainline repository can create missing local actor state without making repeated init calls succeed after identity exists.
summary.why: Cloned repositories already contain team configuration but still need local actor identity before agents can record and publish intents.
summary.user_goal: Use the standard mainline workflow on a fix branch and correct init semantics around local identity.

decisions.chose: Allow init only when team config exists and identity is missing
decisions.rationale: Repository-level setup remains one-time while per-clone actor setup can still be bootstrapped.

decisions.chose: Return an error instead of overwriting malformed identity state
decisions.rationale: Only a missing identity file should be treated as a bootstrap case.
The implementation writes this local actor config and it should not be committed.

rejected.reason: That conflicts with the expectation that repeated init after local identity exists reports already initialized.

risks: Bootstrap Makefile targets that call init repeatedly will now surface already-initialized behavior once local identity exists.
followups: Consider splitting repository init and local identity init into separate commands.

architectural_claims: Repository-level mainline configuration and local actor identity have distinct initialization semantics.
behavioral_changes: mainline init can create local identity when .mainline/config.toml exists but identity is absent.
mainline init returns ALREADY_INITIALIZED when local identity already exists.
Malformed identity read errors are not silently overwritten.
